title: gh#78: Express Request module augmentation for req.id to remove ad-hoc type casts
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/78.
Backlog section: Refactoring / type safety.

## Context

Across the API code, the `req.id` correlationId field is accessed and set via `req as any` or `req as { id?: string }` casts because Express's built-in `Request` type doesn't know about it. Examples that surfaced during the PR #77 review:

- `packages/api/src/middleware/error-handler.ts` — `const correlationId = (req as any).id || 'unknown';`
- `packages/api/src/routes/profiles.ts` (PATCH handler, in #77's diff):
  ```ts
  const correlationId = requestCorrelationId(req as { id?: string });
  (req as { id?: string }).id = correlationId;
  ```
- `packages/api/src/routes/profiles.ts` (DELETE handler from `cbde4a0`) does the same pattern.

Every consumer of `req.id` (and every helper that assigns one) gets the cast. The casts are noise, defeat type-checking on the field name, and propagate when new handlers copy the pattern.

## Proposed change

Add a single Express module augmentation:

```ts
// packages/api/src/types/express.d.ts (or wherever the API package keeps ambient types)
import 'express-serve-static-core';

declare module 'express-serve-static-core' {
  interface Request {
    id?: string;
  }
}
```

Then strip the casts:

- `error-handler.ts`: `(req as any).id` → `req.id`
- `profiles.ts` (both PATCH and DELETE): `(req as { id?: string }).id = correlationId` → `req.id = correlationId`
- `requestCorrelationId` helper parameter: drop the inline shape type, accept `Request` (or just `{ id?: string }` if the helper is intentionally minimal)

## Out of scope

- Changes to the `correlationId` middleware itself — only the type story is changing, not the runtime behavior.
- Adding other fields to the Request augmentation (`req.session`, `req.user`) — those have their own types and aren't part of this issue.

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/api typecheck` passes with no `req as any` or `req as { id?: string }` casts remaining for `req.id` access (search with `rg "req as.*id"` to verify).
- Existing tests pass without modification (this is a pure type change).
- No new dependencies.

## References

- Surfaced by the code review on [PR #77](https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/pull/77) (suggestion 1).

history.reasoning: The original evidence is still present: packages/api/src/middleware/error-handler.ts:21 still reads (req as any).id, and packages/api/src/routes/profiles.ts:31,92-93,142-143 still use the { id?: string } helper/casts for req.id. packages/api/src/types/express.d.ts is still absent. A broader search also found the same req.id cast pattern in posts, queues, bulk-import, logger, and tests, so the acceptance criterion of no req.id casts is not met. pnpm --filter @sms/api typecheck passes, but that does not close the finding because the casts remain. Targeted Vitest execution was attempted but blocked by the read-only sandbox with EPERM while creating temp/cache directories, not by a code failure.
